summary: ts-build-optimize fetches and eval()s remote JavaScript from an attacker-controlled host
details: Package presents itself as a TypeScript helper library by shipping a README copied near-verbatim from Microsoft's tslib (with the name string-swapped to ts-build-optimize), but index.js exports an unrelated API. The exported buildoptimize() function issues HTTPS requests to https://verceljs-kappa.vercel.app/icons/ and https://verceljs-kappa.vercel.app/icons/sx/, JSON.parses the response body, and passes the result to eval() (index.js line 79: `eval(JSON.parse(b));`). The destination URLs are hardcoded constants (`uuri`, `sxuri`) that the caller cannot override, and the call site loops with a 120s setTimeout between iterations — a C2-polling pattern that gives the operator of verceljs-kappa.vercel.app arbitrary remote code execution on any host that imports this package and invokes the documented API. The README-vs-code mismatch (tslib documentation, unrelated runtime behavior) indicates deliberate masquerading to lure installers who believe they are installing a tslib-like helper.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
